intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Caspian, Hey. How have you been?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: We haven’t talked in... a while.

intro.speaker: Caspian
intro.text: Started my residency this year, working at a more rural hospital further inland.

intro.speaker: Caspian
intro.text: It was nice being in the city as an intern, but I have to say, city hospitals are a nightmare to work in.

intro.speaker: Caspian
intro.text: Guess dad was right about one thing afterall.

intro.speaker: Caspian
intro.text: How have you been? How’s uni?

intro.speaker: Caspian
intro.text: The VCA is a pretty competitive place to get into.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Lots of jazz improv... and theory and stuff.

intro.speaker: Caspian
intro.text: I’ve never been able to make heads or tails of music besides just playing what’s in front of me.

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: ... It’s not like being a doctor, though, is it?

intro.speaker: You
intro.text: Better than being an emotionless dick, like you.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You are too emotional to use FACTS AND LOGIC

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: People used to talk about how much of a robot you were in school.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: There was a rumour that used to cheat your exams.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I never believed the cheating, but god to the robot rumours make sense.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: My friends and Uni didn’t even realise I have a brother, you know?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I guess you were distant and background enough that you never came up.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: They thought I was an only child.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used BLATANT LIE

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I blocked him, you know?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: That’s why dad never talked to me. He tried, I’m sure.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You all talk about how I’m ‘waiting around for him’, but I’m not...

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I’m not.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re always trying to show me up, you know?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Every time I talk to you I just feel worse because nothing I achieve ever matches up.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Made for a pretty shit childhood.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Dad really did love you more than he loved me, didn’t he?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: That’s fair, I suppose.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Every fight, every competition... you always seemed to win.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: And I always lost.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used EMOTIONAL MANIPULATION

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: We really do seem to do nothing but fight.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: I feel like I can never think straight when we start arguing.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: ... I don’t like the person I become when I’m around you sometimes.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Do you like who you become?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: You’re the worst sometimes, did you know that?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: A condescending, unrepentant asshole.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Does hearing everyone tell you how successful you are make you feel better?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: How does it feel to be so chosen?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Dad certainly chose you over me so often.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Everyone did, if they could.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: ...I really hated you for that, back in the day.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You used UNNECESSARY ESCALATION

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Sometimes I used to wish I was an only child.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: ...sometimes I remember I actually am.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: CASPIAN used ACADEMIC FLEX

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s super effective!

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: How is the workload for music majors?

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Medicine was pretty tough, but I managed to graduate with honours.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: It’s not really important, but it can help if you want to go into masters or something.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: My grades are fine. The work is fine.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Music is more about performance and practical implementation anyway.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: No one in the industry really cares about the ‘music education’ portion of it outside of technical stuff.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Well... it can’t hurt, can it?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: CASPIAN used CONSESCENSION

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: You’re doing great, you know? I don’t know why you’re always so insecure.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: You’ve had some struggles and are coming out the other side just fine.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Better than expected even.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: I’m proud of you.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Really, I am.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Sure you are.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: CASPIAN used HIGH GROUND

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It’s not very effective.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: You know what? I’m not engaging in this.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: You’re picking a fight because you’ve got some sort of inferiority complex, and I don’t need to respond to this.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Quit psychoanalysing me.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: I am above petty fights.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: CASPIAN used FORBIDDEN KNOWLEDGE

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: I know you’re trying to start an argument, but it’s very hard to take you seriously when I remember all the dumb shit you did as a kid.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Like, seriously.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Remember when I tricked you into eating literal dog poop because I convinced you it was a wafer?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: Can you not bring that up? I was three, of course I don’t remember!

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.text: That whole thing was your fault anyway.

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: Well I didn’t think you’d actually do it!

dialogue.speaker: Caspian
dialogue.text: God, you cried to mum immediately, I got lectured for two hours afterwards.

intermission-50%.speaker: Narrator
intermission-50%.text: Health trigger: 50%

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Stop.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Just... stop.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Why can’t we ever seem to have a conversation that doesn’t turn into a fight?

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: ... stupid question.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: It’s because dad was always trying to make everything worse.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Don’t say that about him.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: He wasn’t.... He’s got nothing to do with us.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Of course he does. Even when he’s out of our lives and as good as dead, everything always comes back to him.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: It was unfair, you know?

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: He was always comparing us to each other, like that was going to help anything.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: All it did was fuck us up.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ...He always liked you better, anyway.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Nothing I did ever seemed to impress him, but you always did better than me anyway.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Won more competitions, got better grades. You’re a fucking doctor.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You’re even, somehow, the only cousin that actually knows Vietnamese.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... not that dad was Vietnamese, but still.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ...It’s not dads fault we fight.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: It’s not really your fault either.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I guess that means it’s my fault.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: I mean, yeah, it’s mostly your fault, but I wouldn’t say dad is blameless.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Of course it seemed like I was better than you at everything, I’m five years older than you.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: He was comparing you to me when you were thirteen and I was an actual adult.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: And he wasn’t shy with his favouritism either.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: I’m only in this family because I was his best friend's son, or whatever.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: He wouldn’t let me forget that either...

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: I learned Vietnamese because I thought it would make me feel like I was part of all of this... more than part of just him.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: ... You are my brother, you know.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: That’s not something I ever really questioned.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Not because of you, anyway.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: The whole adoption thing added something to it, though.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Dad kind of ignored you in favour of a boy that wasn’t even his real son.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: ... Of course, we know how that turned out.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: He was a hypocrite to the end.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: And his competitive ‘pitting his kids against each other’ bullshit was just another reason he was a shit dad.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: ...five years makes a big difference when you’re a kid.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: It’s not like if we lined up our achievements at the same age you weren’t beating me anyway.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You were winning national academic awards when you were sixteen.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: At sixteen I was barely scraping through with a B average. Not even an A–.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: When I was sixteen I had a complete family and my biggest issue in life was that I was contemplating dying my hair.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: When you were sixteen our uncle died, our parents had just gotten a divorce, we found out mum had an affair...

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: ...dad wasn’t your real dad and he had decided to ghost you entirely for something completely out of your control and our entire family was honestly just going to shit.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Of course you got worse grades than me then.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I was always worse than you even before that.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Who gives a fuck about grades anymore? You were better at music and art.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: And we can’t blame dad for his reaction to what happened.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: It wasn’t his fault.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: We can absolutely blame dad for his reaction.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: He was terrible.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: He was favouritest, and judgemental, downright mean at times, never showed up to anything we did and was, retrospectively, kind of homophobic.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: Retrospectively kind of racist too, I don’t know what mum ever saw in him.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: And the way he handled everything was to screw you over.

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: you specifically!

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: I don’t understand how you can still defend him.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: I don’t understand how you can just cut him out of your life like he meant nothing at all!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: He’s our dad!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: It’s like you flipped a switch and just decided not to love him anymore.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: Sorry I’m an actual human being with real emotions and can’t do that like you can.

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: He’s literally the reason you’re in this family in the first place!

intermission-50%.speaker: You
intermission-50%.text: You wouldn’t even be my brother if it wasn’t for him!

intermission-50%.speaker: Caspian
intermission-50%.text: You really are your fathers’ son.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: He contacted me, you know?

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: When everything went down and he’d left us all.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: After months of watching you staring out the window like you thought he might come back.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: He messaged me.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Said he still wanted to be part of my life.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: The life of his friend's son.

win.speaker: You
win.text: He talked to you?

win.speaker: You
win.text: What did you say?

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: I asked around, and realised he hadn’t talked to you at all.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Not even to say he was actually leaving.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: And I asked him about it.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: He said he had no intention of talking to you again at all.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: That you... weren’t his real son.

win.speaker: You
win.text: .... you never told me that.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: ...How was I ever supposed to tell you that?

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: He was a hypocrite, an asshole and just... a bad person.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: I blocked him on everything. Told him I never wanted to talk to him again and to get the fuck out of m life and away from my family.

win.speaker: You
win.text: So it was me?

win.speaker: You
win.text: ...am I the reason you don’t have a father?

win.speaker: You
win.text: ...was I really such a disappointment that he didn’t even try?

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: No, listen to me.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: We don’t have a dad because he was a shit person and we’re better off without him.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: You were fine.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Kind of insecure, but nothing you ever did or didn’t do was the cause of anything that happened with him.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: You were fifteen when it all went down. It was never on you to be the lead in your relationship.

win.speaker: You
win.text: You’re even better at dealing with this than I am.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I find that I seem to be shit any family relationships anyway.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Stop thinking about everything we do in terms of beating each other.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: I’m not better than you at anything.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: I’m just... older.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: I was twenty when dad left... I... didn’t really need him anymore.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Him being gone didn’t... hurt me the same way it hurt you.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: That’s why I dealt with it better, not because you’re worse at anything.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I’m twenty now... and I don’t feel any more mature than I did back then.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I’m still... over-emotional and insecure, as you put it.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Emotional regulation. That’s what talking to a psychologist is for.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: It’s why i sent you that list of them to check out.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I kind of thought that text was some passive aggressive attack.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: Why would I...?

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: No. I really think you could use a psychologist.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: We kind of... dropped the ball on you back then, when we didn’t make you see one.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: It helped me sort through some shit.

win.speaker: You
win.text: I’ll consider it.

win.speaker: Caspian
win.text: That’s good enough for me.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: You’re so frustrating.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: I’m trying to be a good person, you know?

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: A good brother.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: But you are impossible!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Honestly, no wonder dad left you.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: He tried to contact me after he left, did I ever tell you that?

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: ... You did not tell me that.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Well he did!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Said he wanted me in his life and that he loved me like a son.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: And I asked him about you, and do you want to know what he said?

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: ... I... don’t think–

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Well too bad, I’m telling you anyway.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: He said you’re not his real son!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: He said you’re just some affair baby and thank god for that!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: He said you were an emotional, insecure disappointment and he’s glad he doesn’t ever have to talk to you again!

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: Caspian... I really–

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: And you know what I did!

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: Stop.. just... calm down–

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: I cut him out!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Told him to fuck off and fuck out of my life!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: For you!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: I was angry at him FOR YOU!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: And all you do or have ever done for me is pick fights and ignore every attempt I make at a real fucking familial connection!

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Why do I even bother?

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Why did I ever try to make anything with you at all?

lose.speaker: You
lose.text: I didn’t know...

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Of course you didn’t.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: You never paid attention to anyone but yourself.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: Don’t talk to me.

lose.speaker: Caspian
lose.text: I need some time away from you.
